protocol_name: Broth Microdilution Antimicrobial Susceptibility Testing for Aerobic Bacteria
paper_source: CLSI M07: Methods for Dilution Antimicrobial Susceptibility Tests for Bacteria That Grow Aerobically

sequential_steps.notes: Prepare 2-fold dilutions of 10X antimicrobial stock solutions in broth or sterile water depending on drug solubility.
Dispense antimicrobial agent into wells at 2X the desired final concentration; include a growth control well with no antibiotic.
Seal panels in plastic bags and store at <= -20°C, preferably <= -60°C, for later use. Storage duration not precisely defined.
Prepare inoculum from 3-4 well-isolated colonies from an 18-24 hour culture on non-selective media using saline, sterile water, or tryptic soy broth.
Thaw frozen microdilution panels completely to room temperature; inoculate within 4 hours of thaw and do not refreeze.
Add 50 µL inoculum to each well containing 50 µL antibiotic solution, giving 100 µL total volume and approximately 5 x 10^5 cfu/mL final concentration per well.
Inoculate plates within 15 minutes and incubate panels within 15 minutes of inoculation.
Reference BMD incubation for many non-fastidious organisms is 35 +/- 2°C in ambient air for 16-20 hours. Exact duration and atmosphere vary by organism and drug-bug combination.

extraction_notes: The source summarizes CLSI M07 and workshop slides rather than providing a single complete machine-executable protocol; only explicitly stated steps were extracted.
Well positions or deck locations are not provided in the source, so source_location and destination_location are null for all steps.
No specific Opentrons labware name is stated; a generic 96-well plate was selected as the closest match to broth microdilution panels, but this is an inferred hardware mapping.
No specific pipette model is stated; p300_single_gen2 was selected because 50 µL transfers are described, but this is an inferred hardware mapping.
Storage step for frozen panels includes temperatures but not a precise time; duration_seconds is null.
Room temperature is mentioned for thawing but no numeric temperature is given; temperature_celsius is null for thawing-related incubation steps.

Multiple organism-specific media modifications are described (eg, +2% NaCl, +0.002% Tween-80, additional calcium, iron-depleted CAMHB), but exact use depends on organism/drug combination and was not assigned to separate automated steps.
Incubation conditions vary by organism: some require 20-24 hours, 24 hours, CO2, or anaerobic conditions; only the general 35°C ambient air 16-20 hour condition was captured as the default step.

The source includes broth macrodilution, broth microdilution, and agar dilution methods; this extraction focuses on broth microdilution because it is the most directly translatable to a 96-well automated protocol.

MIC reading conventions and interpretation rules are described, but endpoint reading is observational rather than an Opentrons liquid-handling action and therefore not represented as a sequential step.
